title: Shamsul Haque voter registration records and voting records

communications.subject: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: Shamsul Haque voter registration records and voting records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records:

All voter registration records (including past party affiliations) and primary, general and special election voting histories for Shamsul Haque, dating back to 1995. Shamsul Haque's date of birth is 10/24/1972. Haque is currently registered at an address in Queens County: 32-56 69th Street, Woodside, NY 11377.

Other names for Shamsul Haque may include "Shamsul Hague" or "Shansul Haque". 

I am requesting this voter registration data to be used for an “elections purpose” per New York State Election Law Section 3-103(5). Specifically: Shamsul Haque is running as a candidate in the upcoming June 2026 primary election, and I am researching him as part of voter research towards educational outreach.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Sumana Harihareswara

communications.subject: EXTERNALRE: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: Shamsul Haque voter registration records and voting records
communications.communication:
BOARD OF ELECTIONS IN THE CITY OF NEW YORK Dear Sir/Madam: This is an automatically
generated response to your recent e-mail message. The Board of Elections in the City of New York (Board)
acknowledges receipt of your Freedom of Information Law (FOIL) request. Be advised, FOIL requests are processed in the order in which they
are received. Additionally, Board staff are currently focused on fulfilling the
Board’s statutorily mandated pre and post-election activities for the
April Special Election in the 3rd NYC Council District, including building
and testing the ballot, ensuring poll sites are selected, properly
equipped and staffed, and in compliance with all necessary legal requirements,
conducting nine days of early voting, the rolling canvass, the post-election
canvass, completing all required audits, and the ultimate certification of the
election results. Further, the Board is completing its required activities to
prepare for the 2026 Primary and General Elections, including the
imminent filing period for election documents, corresponding cures,
objections, and subsequent ballot access hearings for the June 2026 Primary
Election. This important statutory function of the Board requires the full
attention of staff to fulfill the Board's legal mandates regarding the
intake and review of filed election documents, processing of objections
filed to specific candidacies, and all related litigation. Based on the foregoing and the estimated time it may take to
search, sort, compile and review the records being requested, it is anticipated
the assembly of any available documents will be completed on or before ninety
days (90) days following the date of this notice. Please note, this timeframe
may be modified depending on changing conditions. Your request will be forwarded to the appropriate staff as soon as
possible. When staff is available, a search for any responsive records will be
conducted and the results will be sent to the Records Access Office. You will
be contacted in writing and advised if any records responsive to your request
are located and, if subject to disclosure, when they will be available for
review. Should you request copies of such records, pursuant to the New
York State Public Officers Law § 87(1)(b)(iii), there is a fee of 25¢ per
photocopy of documents nine inches by fourteen inches or less. For larger
documents, the actual cost of reproduction will be charged in accordance with
Public Officers Law § 87(1)(c). The BOE accepts cash, money order, or a corporate check made
payable to the Board of Elections in the City of New York. Please be prepared
to pay for the reproduction costs, if any, when you pick up the materials. If your request is denied you may appeal this determination to the
Board’s FOIL Appeals Officers. Please see the Board’s “Records Access Policy –
FOIL Rules,” which is located on the Board’s website, for additional
information regarding FOIL appeals. 
 Please note, NYS Election Law § 3-103(5) prohibits using
information derived from voter registration records for non-election purposes.
Any person who knowingly and willfully violates this provision is guilty of a
misdemeanor (NYS Election Law § 17-168). 
 
 Records Access Office of the Board of Elections in the City of New
York

title: Flock Email Requests (Oshkosh Police Department)

communications.subject: Wisconsin Open Records Act Request: Flock Email Requests (Oshkosh Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Wisconsin Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Please provide all records of email communications, including attachments and calendar invitations, that were sent to or received from the domain “@flocksafety.com,” between November 1, 2025 and the date this request is fulfilled.

This request includes:
- Emails where @flocksafety.com appears in the sender, recipient (To, CC, BCC), or within the body of the message
- Attachments of any file type, including but not limited to PDFs, presentations, spreadsheets, and images
- Calendar invitations, meeting requests, and related responses involving Flock Safety personnel

Please search all agency-controlled email systems and accounts reasonably likely to contain responsive records, including individual officer accounts, group mailboxes, and archived or backup systems. Records should be provided in native electronic format where possible (e.g., .pst, .msg, or .eml with metadata preserved), including all available metadata fields such as sender, recipients, timestamps, and subject lines.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Alan J. Segal

communications.subject: RE: Wisconsin Open Records Act Request: Flock Email Requests (Oshkosh Police Department)
communications.communication:
Good Afternoon,

In processing your request, it is noticed that this is a large volume request. Is there a way you can narrow the scope of your request?

Thank you,

Penny Knoll
Records Supervisor - Police Department
City of Oshkosh

communications.subject: RE: Wisconsin Open Records Act Request: Flock Email Requests (Oshkosh Police Department)
communications.communication:
Hi Penny,

Not a problem - let me know if the below helps to reduce the volume:
1) Move the start date from Nov 1 to Dec 1 2025
2) De-duplicate license plate hits (If there are 500 automated email that a plate on a list was seen by a camera, I only need one example email, not every single email of every single hit). 
3) De-duplicate any newsletters or mass emails received by multiple individuals (only need 1 copy of each email)

I hope this helps. Thank you!

Best,
Alan

communications.subject: RE: Wisconsin Open Records Act Request: Flock Email Requests (Oshkosh Police Department)
communications.communication:
Good Morning,

Reducing the request by one month still produces a large volume of data. Can you narrow down the request to list specifically what you are looking for?

Thank you,

Penny Knoll
Records Supervisor - Police Department
City of Oshkosh

communications.subject: RE: Wisconsin Open Records Act Request: Flock Email Requests (Oshkosh Police Department)
communications.communication:
Hi Penny,

We are looking for emails between individuals at Flock Safety and Oshkosh PD's command staff, including Chief Smith and other leadership/technology personnel involved in the Flock implementation in Oshkosh. I am interested in emails chains where Flock Safety made statements about their product's capabilities and lawful use of the product. I know Flock sends automated email alerts and newsletters - I am not seeking those types of emails. 

If that does not narrow down the responsive documents, then we can update this request to only include emails that include Chief Smith (To/From/CC/BCC) and Flock Safety, with the understanding that I may make a second request for additional emails between Flock Safety and others at your agency. 

Thank you for the help.

All the best,
Alan

communications.subject: RE: Wisconsin Open Records Act Request: Flock Email Requests (Oshkosh Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

I wanted to follow up on the following Wisconsin Open Records Act request, copied below, and originally submitted on April 20, 2026. Please let me know when I can expect to receive a response.

Thanks for your help, and let me know if further clarification is needed.

communications.communication:
Good Morning,

Please find attached the response to your records request. Let me know if you would like to proceed.

Thank you,

Penny Knoll
Records Supervisor - Police Department
City of Oshkosh

communications.subject: RE: Wisconsin Open Records Act Request: Flock Email Requests (Oshkosh Police Department)
communications.communication:
Good morning Penny, can I please narrow this request to include only emails in Chief Smith's maialbox that include to/from Flock Safety (including when he is CCed or BCCed) from March 1 to April 16? This would narrow the dates significantly, narrow the search down to one mailbox, and still exclude any automated alerts from the system since I'm only looking for person-to-person emails. I hope that narrows the request sufficiently to avoid the high fees. Thank you,
Alan

Please find attached the response to your records request. Let me know if you would like to proceed.

Thank you,

Penny Knoll

communications.communication:

Sunrise Elementary School Principal Michael B. Griffin and Moorlands Elementary School Principal Megan S. Griffin – Cocaine and guns

https://www.wftv.com/news/trending/cocaine-loaded-gun-lead-two-elementary-principals-being-placed-administrative-leave/6XT7SFY6TBB5HNZIOCKDHIG6MU/


PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST RCW 42.56:

I request any/all public records of the following:

1.	COCAINE – Use of cocaine by Northshore School District Elementary School Principals or teachers on or off elementary school grounds.
2.	FIREARMS/HANDGUNS – Names of all Northshore School District Elementary School Principals or teachers in possession of HANDGUNS or FIREARMS on elementary school grounds.
3.	KIDNAPPING – any records or police reports referencing KIDNAPPING and current or former Northshore School District Elementary School Principals or teachers.
4.	RESISTING ARREST - any records or police reports referencing RESISTING ARREST by current or former Northshore School District Elementary School Principals or teachers.
5.	CARJACKING - any records or police reports referencing CARJACKING and current or former Northshore School District Elementary School Principals or teachers.
6.	DRIVING UNDER THE INFLUENCE OF DRUGS OR ALCOHOL - any records or police reports referencing DRIVING UNDER THE INFLUENCE OF DRUGS OR ALCOHOL by current or former Northshore School District Elementary School Principals or teachers.
7.	HIT AND RUN - any records or police reports referencing HIT AND RUN by current or former Northshore School District Elementary School Principals or teachers.
8.	OBSTRUCTION - any records or police reports referencing OBSTRUCTION by current or former Northshore School District Elementary School Principals or teachers.
9.	CRIMINAL or CIVIL CHARGES - copies of all CRIMINAL or CIVIL CHARGES of any current or former Northshore School District Elementary School Principals or teachers.
10.	GUILTY PLEAS – copies of all STATEMENT OF DEFENDANT ON PLEA OF GUILTY entered into by any former Northshore School District Elementary School Principals or teachers.
11.	COMPLAINTS - any records referencing COMPLAINTS by the POLICE or the GENERAL PUBLIC about current or former Northshore School District Elementary School Principals or teachers.

Thank you for your interest in obtaining public records held by the King County Sheriff’s Office. Your request has been received and is being processed. Your request was received in this office on February 04, 2024 and given the reference number P131375-020424 for tracking purposes. 
 
Within five (5) business days (excluding holidays) after receiving your request, we will: 
 
 
  * Provide the record(s); 
  * Or Acknowledge your request and give you a reasonable estimate of how long it will take to respond; 
  * Or Deny the request in writing, stating the reasons for the denial (this could also include a denial of part of your request and a granting of the remainder). 
The day the request is received does not count as one of the five (5) days. Weekends and holidays observed by the agency are also excluded in the calculation.

We are experiencing a high volume of open requests, exceeding 1345. Your request requires clarification in order to perform an adequate search for public records and to ensure preservation of records subject to retention. Under the Public Records Act, a requester must ask for “identifiable public records” before an agency is required to respond.  Reference RCW 42.56.080.  Although an agency may not deny a request solely because it is overbroad, the portion of your request for "any/all records" does not satisfy the requirement that a request be for an identifiable public record. Therefore, our response will be based on our interpretation of your request and search terms as outlined below, unless clarification or additional search terms are provided. We will not proceed with research on your request until such clarification is provided. 

We have interpreted your request for “records” as police events. Using the limited information provided (names of principals), we will search our records management system (Mark43), our digital evidence database (Evidence.com), SECTOR (citation/accident report system),  IAPro (internal investigation/complaint system), and CAD (computer aided dispatch) for records responsive to your request. We understand your request to be for any 911 call audio, body worn camera video, CAD reports, in car video, other video, photographs, police reports, and radio transmissions for any KCSO contacts involving the following names you have provided that involve cocaine use, firearms on elementary school grounds, kidnapping, resisting arrest, carjacking, driving under the influence of drugs or alcohol, hit and run, or obstruction for the past five (5) years (01/01/2019-02/04/2024): Michael Benjamin Griffin 
Meghan Suzane Griffin 
Kristin Bailey 
Brandi Doyle 
Jennifer Welch 
Ali Airhart 
Sarah White 
Kate Eicher 
Katy French 
Dr Gregory E Cox 
Geetha George-Shapiro 
Joel Fagundes 
Sam Ramirez 
Sonja Hoeft 
Emily Whitten 
Cathi Davis 
Shannon Thompson 
Megan Lenz 
Sean Schoenfeldt 
Dana Burgess 
Dawn Bowers 
Nancy Johnson Please note, KCSO does not manage or maintain school employment records- we do not keep a list of former and current Northshore School District Elementary School principals and/or teachers. We will only be able to perform a search of our systems for names you have provided. If you can provide the dates of births for these subjects or addresses where incidents may have occurred, it will greatly assist in our research. Regarding the part of your request for " Names of all Northshore School District Elementary School Principals or teachers in possession of HANDGUNS or FIREARMS on elementary school grounds," as mentioned above, KCSO will only be able to search for incidents involving the provided names. KCSO does not have any general documents listing whether the subjects you are seeking records for are in possession of firearms on elementary school grounds. We have interpreted this portion of your request to mean you are seeking records for investigations/complaints involving the listed subjects that involve firearms on school grounds. Regarding your request for civil charges, KCSO does not refer civil charges. For records related to civil charges, please contact the relevant court. Similarly, guilty pleas are court records. Please contact the relevant court or the Prosecuting Attorney's Office (PAO) to request copies of guilty pleas. We will, however, reach out to our Civil Unit for any records involving the names you have provided. If our interpretation of your request is not accurate, or there are additional types of records you are seeking that we have not listed above, please clarify in a response by March 11, 2024, or we will proceed with our research based on our interpretation of your request and search terms as described above. Depending on the results of our search, we may need to respond to your request through a series of installments with a first installment by June 4, 2024. If we are unable to meet this self-imposed deadline, we will let you know. If we are able to provide a response sooner, we will certainly do so. PLEASE NOTE: Per RCW 42.56.120(4) , the KCSO reserves the right to close a records request after 30 days if a requester does not inspect, view, download, and/or pick up the records we provide. There is no obligation for us to continue producing records that are not being collected by a requester. 
RCW 42.56.120(4) states that if an installment of a records request is not claimed or reviewed, the agency is not obligated to fulfill the balance of the request. 
WAC 44-14-04006(1) states that a records request has been fulfilled and can be closed if an entirely unclear request has not been clarified, a request or installment has not been claimed or reviewed, or the requester cancels the request. Please reply to this message or log in to the Online Request System if you have any questions or would like to clarify or cancel this request. 
Sincerely, Janet Arnette | Project/Program Manager II Sheriff's Administration - Policy Direction King County Sheriff's Office (206)477-2620 | www.kingcounty.gov/sheriff 

If you wish to track or respond to this request, please login to the Online Request System.

Please visit our website  and select "What is the cost to obtain records" for more information. Once we have determined the scope of your request and responsive records, we may provide an estimate for fees requiring 10% down prior to processing your request. 
PLEASE NOTE: Per RCW 42.56.120(4), the KCSO reserves the right to close a records request after 30 days if a requester does not inspect, view, download, and/or pick up the records we provide. There is no obligation for us to continue producing records that are not being collected by a requester. 
RCW 42.56.120(4) states that if an installment of a records request is not claimed or reviewed, the agency is not obligated to fulfill the balance of the request. 
WAC 44-14-04006(1) states that a records request has been fulfilled and can be closed if an entirely unclear request has not been clarified, a request or installment has not been claimed or reviewed, or the requestor cancels the request. Please reply to this message or log in to the Online Request System if you have any questions or would like to clarify or cancel this request. Janet Arnette | Project/Program Manager II Public Disclosure Unit | Support Services King County Sheriff's Office (206)477-2620 | www.kingcounty.gov/sheriff 

If you wish to track or respond to this request, please login to the Online Request System.

Please visit our and select "What is the cost to obtain records" for more information. Once we have determined the scope of your request and responsive records, we may provide an estimate for fees requiring 10% down prior to processing your request. website  PLEASE NOTE: Per RCW 42.56.120(4), the KCSO reserves the right to close a records request after 30 days if a requester does not inspect, view, download, and/or pick up the records we provide. There is no obligation for us to continue producing records that are not being collected by a requester. RCW 42.56.120(4) states that if an installment of a records request is not claimed or reviewed, the agency is not obligated to fulfill the balance of the request. WAC 44-14-04006(1) states that a records request has been fulfilled and can be closed if an entirely unclear request has not been clarified,a request or installment has not been claimed or reviewed, or the requester cancels the request. 
Please reply to this message or log in to the Online Request System if you have any questions or would like to clarify or cancel this request. Public Disclosure Unit | Records Services Section King County Sheriff's Office (206)477-2620 | www.kingcounty.gov/sheriff

PLEASE NOTE: Per RCW 42.56.120(4), the KCSO reserves the right to close a records request after 30 days if a requester does not inspect, view, download, and/or pick up the records we provide. There is no obligation for us to continue producing records that are not being collected by a requester. 
RCW 42.56.120(4) states that if an installment of a records request is not claimed or reviewed, the agency is not obligated to fulfill the balance of the request. 
WAC 44-14-04006(1) states that a records request has been fulfilled and can be closed if an entirely unclear request has not been clarified, a request or installment has not been claimed or reviewed, or the requestor cancels the request. PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST RCW 42.56: 

I request any/all public records of the following: 

1. COCAINE – Use of cocaine by Northshore School District Elementary School Principals or teachers on or off elementary school grounds. 
2. FIREARMS/HANDGUNS – Names of all Northshore School District Elementary School Principals or teachers in possession of HANDGUNS or FIREARMS on elementary school grounds. 
3. KIDNAPPING – any records or police reports referencing KIDNAPPING and current or former Northshore School District Elementary School Principals or teachers. 
4. RESISTING ARREST - any records or police reports referencing RESISTING ARREST by current or former Northshore School District Elementary School Principals or teachers. 
5. CARJACKING - any records or police reports referencing CARJACKING and current or former Northshore School District Elementary School Principals or teachers. 
6. DRIVING UNDER THE INFLUENCE OF DRUGS OR ALCOHOL - any records or police reports referencing DRIVING UNDER THE INFLUENCE OF DRUGS OR ALCOHOL by current or former Northshore School District Elementary School Principals or teachers. 
7. HIT AND RUN - any records or police reports referencing HIT AND RUN by current or former Northshore School District Elementary School Principals or teachers. 
8. OBSTRUCTION - any records or police reports referencing OBSTRUCTION by current or former Northshore School District Elementary School Principals or teachers. 
9. CRIMINAL or CIVIL CHARGES - copies of all CRIMINAL or CIVIL CHARGES of any current or former Northshore School District Elementary School Principals or teachers. 
10. GUILTY PLEAS – copies of all STATEMENT OF DEFENDANT ON PLEA OF GUILTY entered into by any former Northshore School District Elementary School Principals or teachers. 
11. COMPLAINTS - any records referencing COMPLAINTS by the POLICE or the GENERAL PUBLIC about current or former Northshore School District Elementary School Principals or teachers.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

The KCSO Public Disclosure Unit is writing to provide an update regarding this request. Sixty-eight (68) remaining incidents located were reviewed for responsiveness, and seven (7) were determined to be responsive to your request. We anticipate providing another progress update - or next installment - to you by 02/10/2026 . If we are unable to meet this self-imposed disclosure date, we will let you know. If we are able to provide a response sooner, we will certainly do so. Your request is subject to fees as allowed under King County Ordinance 18949 and the Public Records Act, RCW 42.56.120 . Please visit our website  and select "What is the cost to obtain records" for more information. Once we have determined the scope of your request and responsive records, we may provide an estimate for fees requiring 10% down prior to processing your request. 
PLEASE NOTE: Per RCW 42.56.120(4), the KCSO reserves the right to close a records request after 30 days if a requester does not inspect, view, download, and/or pick up the records we provide. There is no obligation for us to continue producing records that are not being collected by a requester. 
RCW 42.56.120(4) states that if an installment of a records request is not claimed or reviewed, the agency is not obligated to fulfill the balance of the request. 
WAC 44-14-04006(1) states that a records request has been fulfilled and can be closed if an entirely unclear request has not been clarified, a request or installment has not been claimed or reviewed, or the requestor cancels the request. Please reply to this message or log in to the Online Request System if you have any questions or would like to clarify or cancel this request. The KCSO Public Disclosure Unit processes requests for records created, managed, and maintained for unincorporated King County and our contract partners  . We have located the following record(s) responsive to your request and they have been uploaded as installment # four (4): 
K16341640 CAD report / C16055200 police report K18193073 CAD report / C18026216 police report K21281461 CAD report (C21031679) K21343655 CAD report / C21039884 police report K22319615 CAD report (C22039318) K23033996 CAD report / C23004275 police report K23139626 CAD report / C23018047 police report Please refer to the uploaded exemption log for an explanation of redactions applied. No police reports were written for C21031679 and C22039318, only Traffic Collision Reports (TCRs). The King County Sheriff's Office (KCSO) no longer processes requests for stand-alone Traffic Collision Reports (TCRs). However, you may obtain a copy of the TCR directly from the Washington State Patrol (WSP) , which is the central repository for TCRs. The Washington State Patrol's on-line website is as follows: http://www.wsp.wa.gov; under the Traffic Safety tab at the top, select Collision Reports, then select WRECR. If you have further questions about the Washington State Patrol's service, please review their website. If you have difficulties with your request and need to contact WSP, they can be reached via phone at 360-570-2355 or via email at collisionreports@wsp.wa.gov. There is also an FAQ (Frequently Asked Questions) located at the bottom of the Collision Reports page if you have further questions regarding submitting a request. 
Please log in to the Records Center at the following link to retrieve the record(s): 
Public Records Request - P131375-020424 Once you log in and go to your request, you will find the uploaded record(s) in the "Files" section. If you are using a mobile device and are having trouble downloading or seeing the records, we recommend using a laptop or desktop computer to access the records. To open and review PDF documents you will need an application that can view PDF documents. 
The KCSO is experiencing a high volume of open requests exceeding 1800. We anticipate our response – or next installment – to you by 05/11/2026 . If we are unable to meet this self-imposed disclosure date, we will let you know. If we are able to provide a response sooner, we will certainly do so.  and select "What is the cost to obtain records" for more information. Once we have determined the scope of your request and responsive records, we may provide an estimate for fees requiring 10% down prior to processing your request. 
PLEASE NOTE: Per RCW 42.56.120(4), the KCSO reserves the right to close a records request after 30 days if a requester does not inspect, view, download, and/or pick up the records we provide. There is no obligation for us to continue producing records that are not being collected by a requester. 
RCW 42.56.120(4) states that if an installment of a records request is not claimed or reviewed, the agency is not obligated to fulfill the balance of the request. 
WAC 44-14-04006(1) states that a records request has been fulfilled and can be closed if an entirely unclear request has not been clarified, a request or installment has not been claimed or reviewed, or the requestor cancels the request. Please reply to this message or log in to the Online Request System if you have any questions or would like to clarify or cancel this request. Public Disclosure Unit | Records Services Section King County Sheriff's Office 206-263-2103 | www.kingcounty.gov/sheriff 

If you wish to track or respond to this request, please login to the Online Request System.

The KCSO Public Disclosure Unit processes requests for records created, managed, and maintained for unincorporated King County and our contract partners . We have provided you with four (4) prior installments. We have performed a search of our records systems and located no additional responsive records. Regarding your request for 911 call audio and radio transmissions, the master recordings for the dates of the responsive incidents are past retention and have purged from our system per the Emergency Communications records retention schedule, so no copies of the recordings can be made.  Regarding your request for body worn camera video and in car video, KCSO either did not employ the use of such cameras at the time of the incidents, or the responding officers were not equipped with body worn cameras or in car cameras at the time of these incidents (KCSO did not begin rolling out body worn cameras and in car cameras until the end of March 2023). No other video was located for any of the responsive incidents. No photographs were located for any of the responsive incidents. Using the information provided, as you have indicated above, the KCSO Public Disclosure Unit performed a search in Mark43 (our records management system), CAD (our Computer Aided Dispatch system), SECTOR (the Statewide Electronic Collision & Ticket Online Records program)Evidence.com (our evidence management system), / TraCS (the Traffic and Criminal Software), Stancil (our emergency communications recording system), and IAPro (our professional standards management system) to identify responsive records . Your request is now closed. We do not intend to further address your request based on the search terms provided, and we do not intend to produce further records. Therefore, the Public Records Act one-year statute of limitations, to seek judicial review of our response to your request, has now begun. Please feel free to contact us within thirty days if you have any questions about your request, you would like to provide additional search terms, or if you believe the KCSO should have records responsive to your request. title: CA CVR Data Request (2022, 2024) (Kings County Election Department)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: CA CVR Data Request (2022, 2024) (Kings County Election Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Cast vote record (CVR) data for the following elections:
1. 2022 Nov Midterm Election
2. 2024 Nov General Election

Specifically, for each of these elections, I’m looking for the full anonymized record of votes cast generated by the relevant voting system technology in your County in that year. In case it is helpful to see an example of the type of data I am requesting from your county, Alameda County has these records posted for the 2024 General Election on their Election Information for November 2024 under the name “Cast Vote Records (CVR) Report”.

Please let me know if you need any further clarification on the type of data that I am requesting.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Aleksandra Conevska

title: Open Records Request: Flock audits (Tifton Police Department)

communications.subject: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Open Records Request: Flock audits (Tifton Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

3. Event Log in Flock
The Flock Safety Event Log for the time period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Island County Sheriff's Office)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Island County Sheriff's Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

communications.subject: [External Message Added] Island County public records request #26-211
communications.communication:
Island County ************************************************************************ A message was sent to you regarding record request #26-211: Good morning. This e-mail serves as our acknowledgement to your Public Records Request dated January 29, 2026, pursuant to RCW 42.56.520, in which you asked for requested/stated: “Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.”.” I have done a cursory glance through our Use of Force information as we do not have a report that encompasses the parameters of what you are requesting but instead, I have located approximately 349 Use of Force reports over the timeframe you are requesting, with an estimate page count of 1,542. Of note, these Use of Force reports are only for the patrol side of the Island County Sheriff’s Office and not the Island County Jail.  We process records requests in the order in which they are received, and your request is currently in the queue. I estimate six to eight months  will be required to review the records for responsiveness and possible exemptions and redactions, and to assemble the final collection of documents. I estimate a first installment on or before May 31, 2026. It may help to understand that Island County is comprised of multiple “agencies” for the purposes of the Public Records Act. This is because county government is an amalgam of independently elected officials who maintain exclusive control over their areas of authority, with respect to other county officials. We maintain exclusive control over the records that pertain to the operation of the Island County Sheriff’s Office.  Per RCW 42.56.120, public records requests are subject to payment of reasonable charges for any records provided. The current rates, as established by Island County Ordinance C-46-22 https://islandcountywa.gov/516/Public-Records-Requests (scroll to bottom of page). An estimated cost, currently, is $20.00. For questions or additional information pertaining to this request, please reply to this email or contact our office at (360) 678-4422. Regards,  Mary Kaye Weller Civil/Public Records Clerk Island County Sheriff's Office ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? Reply to this email or sign in to contact staff at Island County.

Technical support: See our help page

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #2019-0820-F
communications.communication:
Thank you for sending the scanned documents.

For citation and tracking purposes, could you please confirm two catalog details about the attached PDF?

1. Does your statement that “these were all of the documents that were located in that folder” refer to the complete contents of the folder identified as “[Alpha Files] - Jeffrey Epstein [OA/ID 4504]” in 2019-0820-F Segment 1 Mark Middleton?

2. Could you please clarify the correct box/folder citation for this folder? The April 10, 2026 response described the folder as within the Mark Middleton files, Box 2 (OA #4504, NARA #3164), while your later email described it as located in Box 4 of 2019-0820-F Segment 1 Mark Middleton. I want to cite the folder accurately.

communications.subject: Re: Freedom of Information Act Request #2019-0820-F
communications.communication:
Dear Mr. Galaxy,

Thank you for contacting the William J. Clinton Presidential Library and 
Museum, a part of the National Archives and Records Administration (NARA). 
I have answered your questions below.

*1) Does your statement that “these were all of the documents that were 
located in that folder” refer to the complete contents of the folder 
identified as “[Alpha Files] - Jeffrey Epstein [OA/ID 4504]” in 2019-0820-F 
Segment 1 Mark Middleton? *  

Answer: Yes. Everything that was scanned and sent to you were all of the 
documents located in the folder entitled "[Alpha Files] - Jeffrey Epstein 
[OA/ID 4504]" within 2019-0820-F Segment 1 Mark Middleton.

*2)* *Could you please clarify the correct box/folder citation for this 
folder? The April 10, 2026 response described the folder as within the Mark 
Middleton files, Box 2 (OA #4504, NARA #3164), while your later email 
described it as located in Box 4 of 2019-0820-F Segment 1 Mark Middleton. I 
want to cite the folder accurately. *

Answer:  The correct box in 2019-0820-F Segment 1 Mark Middleton 
<https://clinton.presidentiallibraries.us/items/show/118574> that contains 
the folder "[Alpha Files] - Jeffrey Epstein [OA/ID 4504]" is *Box 4*.  
Please see the finding aid for this collection at the link provided.  

As for citation purposes, there is no universal format for citing materials 
from this archival depository. However, researchers should include the 
following information in the citation: the type and date of the document, 
the document title (if applicable), the sender and recipient, the complete 
folder title, and the name/processing number of the collection, the segment 
number (if applicable), the series (if applicable), and the box number (if 
applicable). The citation should conclude with reference to the William J. 
Clinton Presidential Library and to the National Archives and Records 
Administration (NARA). I have attached to this email a copy of our citation 
guide, which includes examples of how to cite records from our Library. 

I hope that this information helps with your research, and if you have any 
further questions please do not hesitate to contact us again.

Sincerely,

Jason Kaplan

Archives Technician

William J. Clinton Presidential Library

1200 President Clinton Avenue

Little Rock, AR 72201

clinton.library@nara.gov

501-244-2877

title: Request for Planning Board Meeting Minutes – March 9, 2026

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Request for Planning Board Meeting Minutes – March 9, 2026
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting copies of the minutes for the Planning Board meeting held on March 9, 2026.

Please provide any minutes for this meeting, whether in draft or final form.

If no minutes exist, please confirm that fact in writing.

This request includes any records that document the discussions, deliberations, and actions taken at the March 9, 2026 Planning Board meeting.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Eileen Kelleher

title: Community ALPR Audit (McLean County Sheriff)

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Community ALPR Audit (McLean County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

1. Justification Records
All records used to justify adoption of Flock Safety cameras, including but not limited to: staff reports, city council or county board memoranda, crime analysis reports, presentations, vendor proposals, and any other documents that described the purpose, anticipated benefit, or stated rationale for purchasing or leasing the system.

2. Effectiveness and Use Statistics
All statistical reports, internal summaries, use-justification documents, crime-reduction assessments, or after-action reviews evaluating the effectiveness of Flock Safety cameras since installation. This includes any records prepared in connection with contract renewals.

3. Cost and Financial Records
All financial records related to Flock Safety cameras, including invoices, purchase orders, annual subscription payments, installation costs, and maintenance costs from the date of first installation through the present. Include any records showing federal, state, grant, or civil asset forfeiture funding used to offset costs.

4. Camera Inventory and Placement
The current inventory or deployment list of Flock Safety cameras operated by or accessible to this agency, including number of units and general location descriptions. Also include any written policies, criteria, risk assessments, or internal documents used to determine camera placement locations.

5. Contracts and Legal Agreements
Copies of all active and recently expired contracts, amendments, renewals, data-sharing agreements, memoranda of understanding, and scope-of-work documents between this agency and Flock Safety.

6. Data Access and Network Sharing
(a) The Flock "Organization Audit" report showing all searches made within this agency's Flock system from the date of installation through the date of this request.
(b) The Flock "Network Audit" report showing all searches of this agency's Flock data by any outside agency from the date of installation through the date of this request.
(c) A list of all agencies currently authorized to access this agency's Flock data, including any federal agencies.
Please provide these records in their original electronic, machine-readable format (CSV or spreadsheet).

7. Security Incidents and Unauthorized Access
All records documenting any security incidents, unauthorized access, data breaches, hacking events, or unauthorized sharing of data collected by Flock Safety cameras, including any correspondence with or notifications from Flock Safety related to such incidents.

8. Secretary of State Audit
All records, correspondence, or internal communications related to the Illinois Secretary of State's 2025 audit of ALPR data-sharing practices, including any communications with the Secretary of State's office or Flock Safety regarding compliance.

9. Marketing and Sales Materials
All promotional, descriptive, and marketing materials shared with this agency by Flock Safety or any representative thereof prior to or in connection with the purchase or renewal of the Flock Safety system.

10. Communications with Flock Safety
All emails, letters, or other written communications between agency personnel and any @flocksafety.com email address from January 1, 2022, through the date this request is processed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brad Bell

communications.subject: FOIA Request :: W012201-042626
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
Response: Request to Reduce/Narrow Part of Your Request for Public Records To: Brad Bell Email: 209420-24294863@requests.muckrock.com Address: MuckRock News, DEPT MR209420 263 Huntington Ave Boston MA 02115 From Chief County FOIA Officer: Taylor Williams Response Date: 04/27/2026 
RE: Your FOIA request W012201-042626 received 4/27/2026 All of part of your FOIA request calls for all records falling within a category, and compliance with all or part of your FOIA request, as it is currently written, would be unduly burdensome. We cannot reduce/narrow your FOIA request for you. This message is us extending to you an opportunity to confer with us, pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/3(g), in an attempt to reduce/narrow this FOIA request to manageable proportions. 
Please reduce/narrow your FOIA request specifically as it pertains to your request #10 for "Communications with Flock Safety All emails, letters, or other written communications between agency personnel and any @flocksafety.com email address from January 1, 2022, through the date this request is processed." 
Complying with your request for these records would be unduly burdensome because a search of emails from 1/1/2022 - present with "@flocksafety.com" in the to/from field yielded 3,519 potential results. A separate search of emails from 1/1/2022 - present with "@flocksaftey.com" in the text field yielded 2,145 potential results. his burden to comply with your request outweighs the public interest in the records due to the number of potential results the archiver searches yielded, the time it would take the Chief County FOIA Officer to review each record to see if it is actually responsive to your request, and the time it would take FOIA Officer(s) to review each responsive record and make any necessary redactions. 
Please reduce/narrow this portion of your FOIA request to be manageable. For example, you can reduce/narrow your FOIA request by: 
(1) providing specific email addresses (not just the ending of email addresses) to be searched; and/or 
(2) providing the email addresses, names, or titles of McLean County employees to be searched (Please note our system is unable to conduct a search by department. If you are unable to provide to/from search parameters, the entire County database will be searched); and/or 
(3) providing keywords to search; and/or 
(4) providing a different timeframe to search (Please review the timeframe that has already been searched, as explained above). 
We do not guarantee that your modified FOIA request will be manageable; rather, we will run another search or otherwise determine if it is manageable. 
If you fail to respond to this message, or if you fail to reduce/narrow your request, you should consider this message to be a denial of your request for these records, pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/3(g). You have the right to appeal a denial by a) submitting a written request for review within 60 days of the denial to the Public Access Counselor, Office of the Illinois Attorney General, Public Access Bureau, 500 S. 2nd Street, Springfield, IL 62701 (phone number 1-877-299-3642) or b) filing suit for injunctive or declaratory relief in the McLean County Circuit Court. 
If there are portion(s) of your FOIA request that we are not requesting to be reduced/narrowed, you will receive a separate response to your FOIA request.

RESPONSE TO REQUEST FOR PUBLIC RECORDS 
To: Brad Bell 
Email: 209420-24294863@requests.muckrock.com 
Address: MuckRock News, DEPT MR209420 263 Huntington Ave Boston MA 02115 
From FOIA Officer: Katlin Crow 
Department: COUNTY SHERIFF 
 
Response Date: 05/12/2026 RE: Your Sheriff FOIA request W012201-042626 dated 4/27/2026 
For the following records: 
To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records: 

1. Justification Records 
All records used to justify adoption of Flock Safety cameras, including but not limited to: staff reports, city council or county board memoranda, crime analysis reports, presentations, vendor proposals, and any other documents that described the purpose, anticipated benefit, or stated rationale for purchasing or leasing the system. 

2. Effectiveness and Use Statistics 
All statistical reports, internal summaries, use-justification documents, crime-reduction assessments, or after-action reviews evaluating the effectiveness of Flock Safety cameras since installation. This includes any records prepared in connection with contract renewals. 

3. Cost and Financial Records 
All financial records related to Flock Safety cameras, including invoices, purchase orders, annual subscription payments, installation costs, and maintenance costs from the date of first installation through the present. Include any records showing federal, state, grant, or civil asset forfeiture funding used to offset costs. 

4. Camera Inventory and Placement 
The current inventory or deployment list of Flock Safety cameras operated by or accessible to this agency, including number of units and general location descriptions. Also include any written policies, criteria, risk assessments, or internal documents used to determine camera placement locations. 

5. Contracts and Legal Agreements 
Copies of all active and recently expired contracts, amendments, renewals, data-sharing agreements, memoranda of understanding, and scope-of-work documents between this agency and Flock Safety. 

6. Data Access and Network Sharing 
(a) The Flock "Organization Audit" report showing all searches made within this agency's Flock system from the date of installation through the date of this request. 
(b) The Flock "Network Audit" report showing all searches of this agency's Flock data by any outside agency from the date of installation through the date of this request. 
(c) A list of all agencies currently authorized to access this agency's Flock data, including any federal agencies. 
Please provide these records in their original electronic, machine-readable format (CSV or spreadsheet). 

7. Security Incidents and Unauthorized Access 
All records documenting any security incidents, unauthorized access, data breaches, hacking events, or unauthorized sharing of data collected by Flock Safety cameras, including any correspondence with or notifications from Flock Safety related to such incidents. 

8. Secretary of State Audit 
All records, correspondence, or internal communications related to the Illinois Secretary of State's 2025 audit of ALPR data-sharing practices, including any communications with the Secretary of State's office or Flock Safety regarding compliance. 

9. Marketing and Sales Materials 
All promotional, descriptive, and marketing materials shared with this agency by Flock Safety or any representative thereof prior to or in connection with the purchase or renewal of the Flock Safety system. 

10. Communications with Flock Safety 
All emails, letters, or other written communications between agency personnel and any @flocksafety.com email address from January 1, 2022, through the date this request is processed. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Brad Bell 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2272364/ 
YOUR REQUEST HAS BEEN:  Approved in Part/Denied in Part/No Records in Part A. Partial Approval: The record(s) you requested that have been approved for release are available via the methods indicated by "yes" in this section:

2. The responsive record(s) may be inspected at the COUNTY SHERIFF office. No Approval Explanation:

No Requester Verification Request was sent to you, and you failed to complete this verification within 30 days; therefore, your request is denied pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/3(j). 
4. Other. No SEE EXPLANATION BELOW. Denial Explanation:

title: Administrative Manifest for Clinton Library FOIA 2019-0820-F

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Administrative Manifest for Clinton Library FOIA 2019-0820-F
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

An administrative manifest for Clinton Library FOIA case 2019-0820-F, identified in the Clinton Digital Library as 2019-0820-F Segment 1.
I am requesting administrative and processing records only, not substantive record content, including:

1. The total number of segments processed to date for FOIA case 2019-0820-F.

2. For each segment, the segment number, release date, page count, collection title, and any public Clinton Digital Library item URL or identifier.

3. The current status of any segments still pending, queued, referred to another agency, withdrawn, closed without records, or otherwise unresolved.

4. Any processing summary, finding aid, case log, or release manifest for FOIA case 2019-0820-F.

5. Any FOIA exemptions, Presidential Records Act restriction categories, referrals, consultations, or other processing bases recorded at the segment level.

6. If any of items 1-5 above are already covered by the existing public finding aid at https://clinton.presidentiallibraries.us/items/show/118574, titled "Mark Middleton - Collection Finding Aid," or any related public Clinton Library item, please direct me to those resources and provide only the gaps not covered by published material.

7. If any portion of the information requested above is more efficiently provided through Library reference services rather than formal FOIA processing, I welcome that channel and consent to forwarding this request to reference staff. A reference response in lieu of formal FOIA processing is acceptable for items 1-5.

Identifying information:

- Known Clinton Digital Library item: https://clinton.presidentiallibraries.us/items/show/118574
- Item title: Mark Middleton - Collection Finding Aid
- Identifier: 2019-0820-F Segment 1
- Subject: Middleton, Mark E.
- Collection: Finding Aids - Collection Descriptions & Inventories
- Publisher: William J. Clinton Presidential Library & Museum

This request is intended to clarify the administrative scope of 2019-0820-F before filing any broader content request. A manifest will show whether additional segments exist, whether they are already public, and whether follow-on requests should target only gaps instead of duplicating already-processed material.

I request a waiver of all search and duplication fees under 5 U.S.C. section 552(a)(4)(A)(iii), because disclosure is in the public interest and is likely to contribute significantly to public understanding of government operations, archival processing, and Presidential records access.

If fees are expected despite this request, please notify me before incurring costs above $25.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

pest galaxy

title: FOIA Request: Flock audits

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: FOIA Request: Flock audits
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

3. Event Log in Flock
The Flock Safety Event Log for the time period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

e

Liz Weber – FOIA Officer

communications.communication: The Boone County Sheriff.

communications.subject: RE: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: FOIA Request: Flock audits
communications.communication:
Your request for flock data has been approved.  Please find attached data that is available in reference to this request.  Please note that any information related to open investigations has been redacted and is exempt pursuant to the following:

The Freedom of Information Act has been denied pursuant to The Freedom of Information Act 5 ILCS 140/7(1)(d)(vii) (West 2016)), records that would obstruct an ongoing investigation by the agency that is the recipient of the request.

You have a right to have your request reviewed by the Public Access Counselor (PAC) at the Office of the Illinois Attorney General.  5 ILCS 140/9.5(a).  You can file your Request for Review with the PAC by writing to:

Public Access Counselor
Office of the Attorney General
500 South 2nd Street
Springfield, IL. 62706
877-299-3642

You also have the right to seek judicial review by filing a lawsuit in the State circuit court.  5 ILCS 140/11.

If you choose to file a Request for Review with the PAC, you must do so within 60 calendar days of the date of this letter.  5 ILCS 140/9.5(a).  Please note that you must include a copy of your original FOIA request and this letter when filing a Request for Review with the PAC.

Please note that if this request is for a commercial purpose the FOIA request is not subject to review by the Public Access Counselor, except for the limited purpose of reviewing whether the public body’s determination that the request was made for a commercial purpose is appropriate.  5 ILCS 140/9.5(b).

In addition, as provided under 5 ILCS 140/3.1(c), it is a violation of this Act for a person to knowingly obtain a public record for a commercial purpose without disclosing that it is for a commercial purpose, if requested to do so by the public body.

Liz Weber – FOIA Officer
Boone County Sheriff’s Office
Belvidere Police Department
815-547-1704

weber@boonecountyil.gov<mailto:weber@boonecountyil.gov>

title: CPRA - Ventura County - Flock Safety Documents (Ventura County Sheriff's Department)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: CPRA - Ventura County - Flock Safety Documents (Ventura County Sheriff's Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

- Organization Audit in Flock. 
All data logged from July 1, 2023 to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, this report is available within the Insights tab and reflects searches conducted within the agency.

- Network Audit in Flock. 
All data logged from July 1, 2023 to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, this report is available within the Insights tab and reflects searches of the agency's Flock network by any agency in the Flock system.

- Hardware.
The current number and installed locations of any Flock Safety, LPR,  AVIS, or machine learning-assisted camera systems (provide locations as coordinates or address with nearest cross-street), and any documents related to planned future installations of the same.

- Documents.
>  Invoices, contracts, purchase orders, and bid documents related to the acquisition or renewal of Flock Safety products or services
>  Agreements with homeowner associations, private businesses, or individuals for access to Flock Safety systems
>  Training materials, policies, and SOPs related to Flock Safety platform use
>  Grant or funding records related to the Flock system
>  Data retention policies for data collected by Flock Safety systems
>  Any complaints or grievances received from the public regarding ALPR camera placement or data use


I am not requesting personally identifiable information or license plate data. If any responsive record contains such information, please redact only that portion and produce the remainder. If any records are withheld, please provide a written index identifying each withheld record and the statutory exemption claimed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jordan

title: FIFA Event Approvals (Seattle Office Of Economic Development)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: FIFA Event Approvals (Seattle Office Of Economic Development)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Section 8.17.7(iii) of the City's World Cup Host City Agreement requires that "no other substantial cultural events [...] other than concerts or Host City Event approved by FIFA [...] shall be organised or staged in the Host City within a period commencing one (1) day prior to a Match Day or opening ceremony taking place at a day prior to the Opening Match and ending one (1) day after a match day."

Please provide all communications with FIFA, the Local Organizing Committee, and other parties representing FIFA including but not limited to emails, chat messages (including Microsoft Teams, Slack, Jabber, Zoom, WhatsApp, Signal), text messages, and voice mails regarding events seeking and/or receiving/being denied such approval. Please include any attachments and associated metadata. For chat messages, please provide all messages in the chat within 1 hour for context.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Rose Terse

communications.subject: City of Seattle Public Records Request Center - Invoice Ready :: C249698-121525
communications.communication:
Dear Rose Terse, 
 
Records in response to your public disclosure request C249698-121525 received on December 15, 2025 are now available. The cost for the records is as follows: 
 
Invoice Number: INV26-C249698-1 
Total: $1.25 
 
To pay online, please follow these steps: 
 Please note that you will need to disable any pop-up blockers to make a payment using the system. 
 
  * Navigate to the Public Records Request Center.
  * Select 'My Records Request Center'. 
  * Select 'View My Requests'. You may be asked to login. 
  * Find this request: C249698-121525. 
  * Select the 'View Invoices' button. 
 
  * Opens a pop-up window showing your invoice: INV26-C249698-1. 
  * Click on the invoice and then the 'Pay Now' button. 
  * Continue processing your payment. 
  * Select 'Done'. 
  * Once the payment process is done, you are returned to the Public Records Request Center. 
  * After your payment is successful you will receive an email from our payments center. 
Once your payment has been approved, we will send you an email with instructions on how to download your records. Note that City staff must manually upload records. Records will be available one to two business days after payment. 
 
Please let your public disclosure officer (PDO) know if you are unable to pay online; your PDO will work with you on alternative ways to make your payment. 
 
You have 30 days from today's date to pay for your records. If the City does not receive your payment within this time period, your request will be considered abandoned, and you will need to submit a new request. 
 Please add seattle@mycusthelp.net to your E-mail contacts/address book to ensure delivery of the record center E-mails to your Inbox. (mailto:seattle@mycusthelp.net) 
This is an auto-generated email and has originated from an unmonitored email account. Please DO NOT REPLY.

communications.communication:
Dear Rose Terse, 
 
Records in response to your public disclosure request C249698-121525 received on December 15, 2025 are now available. The cost for the records is as follows: 
 
Invoice Number: INV26-C249698-2 
Total: $1.25 
 
To pay online, please follow these steps: 
 Please note that you will need to disable any pop-up blockers to make a payment using the system. 
 
  * Navigate to the Public Records Request Center. 
  * Select 'My Records Request Center'. 
  * Select 'View My Requests'. You may be asked to login.

  * Select 'Done'.

Once your payment has been approved, we will send you an email with instructions on how to download your records. Note that City staff must manually upload records. Records will be available one to two business days after payment. 
 
Please let your public disclosure officer (PDO) know if you are unable to pay online; your PDO will work with you on alternative ways to make your payment. 
 
You have 30 days from today's date to pay for your records. If the City does not receive your payment within this time period, your request will be considered abandoned, and you will need to submit a new request.

communications.subject: Economic Development :: C249698-121525
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 

Dear Rose Terse, 
Records are now available in response to your request C249698-121525 regarding the following: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

Section 8.17.7(iii) of the City's World Cup Host City Agreement requires that "no other substantial cultural events [...] other than concerts or Host City Event approved by FIFA [...] shall be organised or staged in the Host City within a period commencing one (1) day prior to a Match Day or opening ceremony taking place at a day prior to the Opening Match and ending one (1) day after a match day." 

Please provide all communications with FIFA, the Local Organizing Committee, and other parties representing FIFA including but not limited to emails, chat messages (including Microsoft Teams, Slack, Jabber, Zoom, WhatsApp, Signal), text messages, and voice mails regarding events seeking and/or receiving/being denied such approval. Please include any attachments and associated metadata. For chat messages, please provide all messages in the chat within 1 hour for context. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Rose Terse 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2152234/ 
The records being made available to you at this time include the following: meeting agendas noting an event moratorium. 
To download your records, please: 
 
  * Navigate to the Public Records Request Center. 
  * Select 'My Records Request Center'. 
  * Select 'View My Requests'. You may be asked to login. 
  * Select this request: C249698-121525. 
  * Select the 'View Files' button. 
  * You can download records individually or all at the same time by clicking 'Download All'. 
  *  Please save your records as you may only download them from the system three times. If you do not download your records within 30 days of request closure, the link to your records will expire. 
If you believe you have received privileged or confidential records in error, please notify the City of Seattle immediately, through the Public Records Request Center or by email. Privileged or confidential records provided or received in error should not be reviewed, used, disseminated, distributed, or copied, and should be destroyed immediately. Thank you in advance for your cooperation. 
Due to the size and scope of your request, we are providing records to you in installments. We anticipate that the next installment of records will be available on or about May 1. 
Sincerely, 
Office of Economic Development

communications.subject: RE: Washington Public Records Act Request #C249698-121525
communications.communication:
Hello Kristin,

The Washington Court of Appeals Div I has explained that agencies providing records in installments "must make them with reasonable diligence." C.S.A., 32 Wn. App. 2d at 558

I have once again received only 2 documents with this installment. Would you please provide the strategy being employed for your search? It seems like the logical strategy would be to identify events in the targeted period and review communications regarding the approval process.

With any reasonable search strategy, it seems improbable to have received a total of 21 pages of responsive records, even accounting for any third party notice.

I am once again asking that you provide the full breadth of records I have requested without further delay.

Thank you,
Rose

communications.subject: Economic Development :: C249698-121525
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
Hi Rose, I understand your frustration. The thought process for how the search was conducted was that since we don’t have one place that has denied or permitted events moved for any particular reason, I did a pretty broad keyword search. This brought back thousands of documents that I am sorting through to find any records that relate to event organizers seeking approval or being denied a permit due to the timing of the FIFA World Cup. On top of this, responding to public records requests for the Office of Economic Development is not the only work I’m responsible for, nor is this my only request. I am working diligently to sort through those records and find any responsive records in a timely manner while balancing my other work. If you would like, I can focus the search on what was permitted. Or if there are specific events you are looking for, I can conduct a more focused search. I appreciate your patience as I continue to sort through the records. Cheers, Kristin

communications.subject: RE: Washington Public Records Act Request #C249698-121525
communications.communication:
Hello Kristin,

Appreciate the explanation. Please limit the search to emails, contracts, and other agreements between the city and FIFA regarding cultural events in the time range defined in the Host City Agreement, prioritizing records regarding Trans Pride Seattle scheduled for June 26, 2026 and PrideFest Capitol Hill scheduled for June 27, 2026.

Thank you,
Rose
