title: Request to ATF for all 2025 film and TV collaborations

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Request to ATF for all 2025 film and TV collaborations
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

A list of all the films and television productions that the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives (ATF) has supported, in any capacity whatsoever, during the year 2025. Such a list, full or partial, maybe include, but should not limited to, entertainment liaison (ELO) reports or similar reports. I request that you search for these documents in any location where they might be located, including but not limited to the entertainment liaison offices and communications HQ.

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation as well as the editor-in-chief and a contributor to a research magazine published by said foundation titled "Cultural Engineering Studies." These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply. Attached is a document with organization letterhead confirming my position, as well as the organization's 501(c)(3) application and approval form.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo

title: Flock Safety ALPR Audits (Houston Police Department)

communications.subject: Texas Public Information Act Request: Flock Safety ALPR Audits (Houston Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Flock Shared Networks Information from the period of February 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. 
This should include: "Organization Name", "Networks Shared With Me", "Networks I'm Sharing" from the period of February 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. 
a. The names of agencies and organizations with which the Agency shares ALPR data
b. The names of agencies and organizations from which the Agency receives ALPR data
c. The names of agencies and organizations with which the Agency shares “hot list” information
d. The names of agencies and organizations from which the Agency receives “hot list” information

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Sincerely,

Concerned

communications.subject: Public Information Act Request :: P138879-022526
communications.communication:
Dear Concerned Concerned: 
Thank you for your interest in public records of Houston Police Department. Your request has been received and is being processed in accordance with Chapter 552 of Texas Government Code, the Public Information Act. Your request was received in this office on 2/25/2026 and given the reference number P138879-022526 for tracking purposes. Records Requested: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records: 

Flock Shared Networks Information from the period of February 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. 
This should include: "Organization Name", "Networks Shared With Me", "Networks I'm Sharing" from the period of February 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. 
a. The names of agencies and organizations with which the Agency shares ALPR data 
b. The names of agencies and organizations from which the Agency receives ALPR data 
c. The names of agencies and organizations with which the Agency shares “hot list” information 
d. The names of agencies and organizations from which the Agency receives “hot list” information 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Concerned 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2226525/ 
Your request will be forwarded to the relevant Houston Police Department department(s) to locate the information you seek and to determine the volume and any costs associated with satisfying your request. You will be contacted about the availability and/or provided with copies of the records in question. PLEASE NOTE: The Chapter 552 of Texas Government Code, the Public Information Act does not require a governmental body to create new information, to do legal research, or to answer questions. 
 
You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you'll receive an email when your request has been completed. Again, thank you for using the Houston Public Information Request Center. 
Houston Police Department

title: Efforts to remove books from Naval Academy library (Department of Defense, Office of the Secretary of Defense)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Efforts to remove books from Naval Academy library (Department of Defense, Office of the Secretary of Defense)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Any document concerning the disposition, destruction, displacement, or removal of books from the Naval Academy library.

2. Any document reflecting the search terms utilized to remove books from the Naval Academy library.

3. Any communications to or from the Office of the Secretary of Defense concerning books at the Naval Academy library. 

4. Any communications to or from Vice Adm. Yvette Davids which mentions any of the following terms: library, book, books, DEI, diversity, EO, executive order, gender, ideology. This request does not include copies of news reports.

5. Any communications to or from Cmdr. Tim Hawkins which mentions any of the following terms: library, book, books, DEI, diversity, EO, executive order, gender, ideology. This request does not include copies of news reports.

These requests are limited to records generated on or after January 20, 2025.

"Communications" means and includes emails, text messages, social media messages, letters, memoranda, calendar entries, chats, instant messages, or any other form of one-to-one or one-to-many communication. "Communications" also includes transcripts, videos, audio, minutes, notes, or other recordings of conversations, meetings, phone calls, or Zoom (or similar) meetings.

Please note that this request seeks public records wherever they may be found, including on devices or networks owned or operated by private parties. For example, if an employee uses a personal cell phone or social media account to conduct public business, records created, maintained, or held on the phone or account are public records subject to this request.

REQUEST FOR EXPEDITED PROCESSING:

I request that this request be expedited under 32 CFR § 286.8(e)(1)(i)(B) for the following reasons:

1. I certify the following to be true and correct: My organization is primarily engaged in disseminating information. The Foundation for Individual Rights and Expression (FIRE) maintains a news website at https://thefire.org/news, through which its staff members regularly publish news stories and editorial content concerning the activities of governments, government officials, and other persons or organizations of public concern. I am a staff member of FIRE. I frequently write about matters involving the First Amendment and press freedom, including on FIRE's website. See, e.g., https://www.thefire.org/news-archive?author=303. A substantial component of my duties as a staff member of FIRE is to obtain information through public records requests, including through the Freedom of Information Act, which I or my colleagues then share through FIRE's website and social media channels. I also publish news and other information of public concern via "X" (https://x.com/adamsteinbaugh) and "Bluesky" (https://bsky.app/profile/adamsteinbaugh.bsky.social).

2. The requested information concerns an actual or alleged government activity, namely the removal of books from a library due to their viewpoints or content.

3. The requested information extends beyond the public's right to know about government activity generally and instead has a particular value that would be lost if not processed on an expedited basis. The information pertains to a breaking news story of public interest. That issue has attracted significant media coverage. The information has value that would be lost if not processed on an expedited basis.

Media coverage evidencing the significant public interest in this matter includes:
https://www.nytimes.com/2025/04/04/us/politics/naval-academy-dei-books-removed.html
https://www.washingtonpost.com/education/2025/04/02/naval-academy-remove-400-dei-books/
https://www.foxnews.com/politics/naval-academy-tosses-400-books-from-library-following-trump-dei-expulsion-orders
https://apnews.com/article/military-academies-dei-hegseth-trump-ba9731f24b4eb4bd9c02b568209f97af
https://blavity.com/jackie-robinson-mlk-books-naval-academy-dei-ban
https://foxbaltimore.com/news/local/naval-academy-will-not-remove-dei-related-materials-from-library
https://dailycaller.com/2025/04/02/naval-academy-removes-hundreds-of-books-that-push-dei/
https://www.washingtonexaminer.com/policy/defense/3367284/naval-academy-removes-400-books-dei-directive-hegseth/
https://www.baltimoresun.com/2025/04/02/hegseth-addresses-naval-academy-ahead-of-reported-book-ban/
https://www.nbclosangeles.com/news/national-international/maya-angelou-memoir-holocaust-book-among-dei-purge-at-naval-academy-library/3671190/
https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2025/apr/03/naval-academy-dei-books
https://thehill.com/homenews/race-politics/5227843-naval-academy-library-books-dei-trump-hegseth/
https://thehill.com/opinion/national-security/5228318-the-navy-cant-fight-for-freedom-while-banning-books/
https://www.thecollegefix.com/u-s-naval-academy-removes-400-books-that-promote-dei-from-library/
https://saraacarter.com/naval-academy-removes-400-books-following-dei-crackdown/
https://www.academia.org/naval-academy-purges-dei-books-from-library/

REQUEST FOR A FEE WAIVER OR REDUCED FEES:

I request, under 32 CFR § 286.12(l), a waiver or reduction of fees for the following reasons:

1. Disclosure of the requested information is in the public interest because its disclosure would shed light on the operations or activities of the government. The requested information pertains specifically to the policies, procedures, and practices of senior Department officials concerning their interaction with the media, implicating the Speech and Press clauses of the First Amendment.

2. Disclosure of the requested information would be likely to contribute significantly to public understanding of those operations or activities. Disclosure of the requested information would meaningfully inform the public about the Department's activities and operations, as the information pertains to the policies and underlying information driving decisions by senior Department officials. Disclosing the policies and other information would demonstrate the bases for those decisions, which have attracted substantial public attention, as demonstrated by the articles and press statements cited above. That information is not in the public domain.

3. Disclosure of the requested information will contribute to the understanding of a reasonably broad audience of persons interested in the subject, as opposed to the individual understanding of the requester. The requestor is a licensed attorney who practices First Amendment law, including its application to members of the press. As such, the requestor will be able to effectively convey information to the public. Further, the requestor is a representative of the news media. The requestor and his employer, the Foundation for Individual Rights and Expression, gather information of potential interest to a segment of the public, turns that information into distinct works (including through its news website, located at https://thefire.org/news), and distributes that information to the public. The information requested here relates to information that is about current events or that would be of current interest to the public. The requestor's past publication record (in addition to FIRE's articles available at https://thefire.org/news) is available at https://www.thefire.org/news-archive?author=303.

4. The requested information is not primarily in the commercial interest of the requester. The requestor works for a nonprofit organization and the records are not sought for a commercial purpose.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Adam Steinbaugh

Ileana Garnand". Due to the increasing number of FOIA requests received by this office, we may encounter some delay in processing your request. title: Community ALPR Audit 2 (McLean County Sheriff)

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Community ALPR Audit 2 (McLean County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

1. Ideological/Identity Based Search Records

All Flock search records (including Organization Audit logs with purpose fields) where the stated reason, tag, or description references any protest, demonstration, march, rally, or political/social justice event or organization or refers to a protected characteristic such as race, gender, nationality, religion, disability, etc. including slang and unredacted slurs.

2. Misuse and Discipline Records

All internal affairs complaints, internal notes, investigative reports, disciplinary records, or personnel actions related to potential misuse of Flock Safety systems (including stalking, personal use, or unauthorized access) from installation to present.

3. False Positives and Wrongful Stops

All records documenting false-positive identifications, misidentifications, erroneous “hot list” alerts, or resulting wrongful stops/detentions tied to Flock data, including internal complaints, incident reports, or legal claims.

4. Flock Raven/Audio Detection Deployment

All records related to any Flock Safety Raven acoustic sensors or audio detection capabilities operated by this agency, including purchase orders, installation records, location lists, and any policies describing audio capture, storage, access, and retention.

5. Audio Recording Legal Analysis

All internal or external legal opinions, memoranda, or analyses regarding whether Flock Raven or similar audio-capable devices comply with Illinois eavesdropping law (720 ILCS 5/14-2), including assessments of potential recording inside vehicles, homes, or locations with a reasonable expectation of privacy.

6. AI Features Usage (FreeForm)

All records describing or referencing the use of Flock’s FreeForm search feature or any AI-assisted search tool by this agency, including training materials, usage policies, internal audits, or guidance on when/how such features may be employed.

7. Warrant vs. Warrantless Search Policy

All written policies, procedures, or guidance addressing whether officers must obtain a warrant or court order before accessing Flock data or conducting Flock searches, including any limitations on historical data searches.

8. External Agency Data Sharing Details

Beyond the basic authorized agencies list, please provide any available details about:
- Specific types of data shared with each external agency
- Frequency or volume of sharing with major recipients (particularly federal agencies)
- Any memoranda of understanding or data-sharing agreements governing external access

9. Internal Search Purpose Transparency

The Flock Organization Audit report showing all internal searches with the full, unredacted “purpose” or “reason” field for each search, in machine-readable format (CSV/spreadsheet), to assess patterns of use and potential discriminatory targeting.

10. Data Retention and Deletion Policies

All records describing this agency’s data retention and deletion practices for Flock Safety data, including:
- Written policies on how long Flock data is retained
- Procedures for deleting or purging data
- Any records of incidents where data was retained beyond stated periods or not properly deleted
- Communications with Flock Safety regarding data retention capabilities or limitations
- Audits or a lack thereof which verify data is actually deleted and shared only as per stated policy

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brad Bell

RE: Your FOIA request W012216-042826 dated 4/29/2026 Your request is too broad as written; therefore, this department is currently unable to conduct a search for the requested records. Please reply to this message and clarify your request by specifying dates in which you are requesting search records. 
If we have not heard from you regarding this request for clarification within 5 business days, we will presume you no longer wish to receive the records and this request will be closed (but can be re-opened if you respond), as we are unable to process it with the information you provided within the request. You have the right to appeal a closed request (i.e., a denial) by a) submitting a written request for review within 60 days of the denial to the Public Access Counselor, Office of the Illinois Attorney General, Public Access Bureau, 500 S. 2nd Street, Springfield, IL 62701 (phone number 1-877-299-3642) or b) filing suit for injunctive or declaratory relief in the McLean County Circuit Court.

communications.subject: RE: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request #W012216-042826
communications.communication:
Thank you for your response dated April 29, 2026. I note that your letter states my request is “too broad as written” but does not specify which particular items you find overbroad, explain why compliance with any item would be unduly burdensome, or identify the specific burden (e.g., volume of records, review time) as required under 5 ILCS 140/3(g).

In the interest of resolving this promptly and in good faith, I offer the following clarifications and date ranges:

For items 1, 2, 3, 8, and 9 (which request search records, discipline logs, false-positive reports, data-sharing details, and audit logs) let us specify the request from the date of Flock Safety system installation through April 30, 2026.

For items 4, 5, 6, 7, and 10, these request current and recently superseded written guidance documents. Please provide all versions of responsive documents currently in effect or that have been superseded since the Flock system’s installation.

If you would identify which item(s) you contend are burdensome, explain the specific burden, and propose a narrowing, we may confer further as the statute requires.

I look forward to your specific response so we may proceed. Per 5 ILCS 140/3(b), I anticipate a response within 5 business days.

RESPONSE TO REQUEST FOR PUBLIC RECORDS 
To: Brad Bell 
Email: 209557-07507955@requests.muckrock.com 
Address: MuckRock News, DEPT MR209557 263 Huntington Ave Boston MA 02115 
From FOIA Officer: Katlin Crow 
Department: COUNTY SHERIFF 
 
Response Date: 05/12/2026 RE: Your Sheriff FOIA request W012216-042826 dated 4/29/2026 
For the following records: 
To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records: 

1. Ideological/Identity Based Search Records 

All Flock search records (including Organization Audit logs with purpose fields) where the stated reason, tag, or description references any protest, demonstration, march, rally, or political/social justice event or organization or refers to a protected characteristic such as race, gender, nationality, religion, disability, etc. including slang and unredacted slurs. 

2. Misuse and Discipline Records 

All internal affairs complaints, internal notes, investigative reports, disciplinary records, or personnel actions related to potential misuse of Flock Safety systems (including stalking, personal use, or unauthorized access) from installation to present. 

3. False Positives and Wrongful Stops 

All records documenting false-positive identifications, misidentifications, erroneous “hot list” alerts, or resulting wrongful stops/detentions tied to Flock data, including internal complaints, incident reports, or legal claims. 

4. Flock Raven/Audio Detection Deployment 

All records related to any Flock Safety Raven acoustic sensors or audio detection capabilities operated by this agency, including purchase orders, installation records, location lists, and any policies describing audio capture, storage, access, and retention. 

5. Audio Recording Legal Analysis 

All internal or external legal opinions, memoranda, or analyses regarding whether Flock Raven or similar audio-capable devices comply with Illinois eavesdropping law (720 ILCS 5/14-2), including assessments of potential recording inside vehicles, homes, or locations with a reasonable expectation of privacy. 

6. AI Features Usage (FreeForm) 

All records describing or referencing the use of Flock’s FreeForm search feature or any AI-assisted search tool by this agency, including training materials, usage policies, internal audits, or guidance on when/how such features may be employed. 

7. Warrant vs. Warrantless Search Policy 

All written policies, procedures, or guidance addressing whether officers must obtain a warrant or court order before accessing Flock data or conducting Flock searches, including any limitations on historical data searches. 

8. External Agency Data Sharing Details 

Beyond the basic authorized agencies list, please provide any available details about: 
- Specific types of data shared with each external agency 
- Frequency or volume of sharing with major recipients (particularly federal agencies) 
- Any memoranda of understanding or data-sharing agreements governing external access 

9. Internal Search Purpose Transparency 

The Flock Organization Audit report showing all internal searches with the full, unredacted “purpose” or “reason” field for each search, in machine-readable format (CSV/spreadsheet), to assess patterns of use and potential discriminatory targeting. 

10. Data Retention and Deletion Policies 

All records describing this agency’s data retention and deletion practices for Flock Safety data, including: 
- Written policies on how long Flock data is retained 
- Procedures for deleting or purging data 
- Any records of incidents where data was retained beyond stated periods or not properly deleted 
- Communications with Flock Safety regarding data retention capabilities or limitations 
- Audits or a lack thereof which verify data is actually deleted and shared only as per stated policy 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Brad Bell 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2274002/

title: RFP #1231 – Williamson County Schools Outsourced Lawncare Services

communications.subject: Tennessee Public Records Act Request: RFP #1231 – Williamson County Schools Outsourced Lawncare Services
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Tennessee Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

At your earliest convenience, I am requesting the following items that resulted from RFP #1231 – Williamson County Schools Outsourced Lawncare Services:
- Final bid tabulations 
- All Executed agreements with awarded vendors (D&D Lawncare, Greenway, Holton Landscapes, and Southbound Erosions) for base and option years
- All names of previous members of the selection committee and associated deliberation notes on award decision

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 7 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Kent Hoover, a citizen of Tennessee, in coordination with Drek Hawkins.

title: ALPR audit

communications.subject: The Missouri Sunshine Law Request: ALPR audit
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the The Missouri Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

Concerning Flock Safety, these files;

1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

Typically the output for these audits is available in monthly batches.

3. A list of network share settings that include:
3a. Flock networks shared with me
3b. Networks that I’m sharing

Please provide records in their original electronic, machine readable format. For example, please provide csv or spreadsheet files where applicable.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Traci

title: Public Records Request — Contracts with Flock Safety and Axon Enterprise / Fusus (Asheville Police Department)

communications.subject: North Carolina Public Records Law Request: Public Records Request — Contracts with Flock Safety and Axon Enterprise / Fusus (Asheville Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the North Carolina Public Records Law, G.S. Chapter 132, I hereby request the following records:

1. All active contracts, agreements, amendments, statements of work, purchase orders, and any associated privacy addenda, data sharing agreements, or terms of service incorporated by reference between the Asheville Police Department and/or the City of Asheville and Flock Safety, including the contract executed in or around February 2025 for automated license plate reader services, and any subsequent amendments.

2. All active contracts, agreements, amendments, statements of work, purchase orders, and any associated privacy addenda, data sharing agreements, or terms of service incorporated by reference between the Asheville Police Department and/or the City of Asheville and Axon Enterprise, Inc., including its subsidiary or acquired platform Fusus, as well as any pending or draft consolidated contract currently under review for City Council consideration.

3. The Memorandum of Understanding between the Asheville Police Department and the Buncombe County Sheriff's Office executed in or around December 2022 relating to access to the Fusus real-time intelligence platform, and any amendments or successor agreements.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. I request a waiver of any fees on the basis that disclosure of these records is in the public interest and the records will be disseminated to the community. 

If any portion of these records is withheld, please cite the specific statutory exemption under G.S. Chapter 132 for each redaction.

I would prefer the request filled electronically (PDF is acceptable) by e-mail attachment, within 10 business days if possible.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Asheville Citizen

title: Lake Geneva Wisconsin Police and Fire Commission meeting recordings

communications.subject: Wisconsin Open Records Act Request: Lake Geneva Wisconsin Police and Fire Commission meeting recordings
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Wisconsin Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

---

To the Custodian of Records,

Pursuant to the Wisconsin Open Records Law, Wis. Stat. §§ 19.31–19.39, I request copies of the **audio recordings of the last five (5) meetings** of the City of Lake Geneva Police and Fire Commission, as of April 23, 2026.

If any of these recordings are maintained in digital format, I request that they be provided in their native electronic format (e.g., MP3, WAV, or similar) via email, file transfer, or other electronic means.

If audio recordings are not available for any of these meetings, please provide:

1. A written explanation stating whether such recordings were created, and
2. If created but no longer available, the reason for their absence (e.g., deletion, retention policy, or other disposition), and
3. Any existing records that serve as substitutes (such as minutes, transcripts, or notes).

If any portion of this request is denied, please cite the specific statutory exemption(s) relied upon and provide the reasons for withholding the record(s), as required by law.

I request a waiver of any fees on the grounds that this request is in the public interest. If fees are expected to exceed $25, please inform me before fulfilling the request.

As required by Wis. Stat. § 19.35(4)(a), I look forward to your response as soon as practicable and without delay.

Please confirm receipt of this request.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Gary Milliette

title: Cybercheck communications & agreements

communications.subject: Ohio Open Records Law Request: Cybercheck communications & agreements
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Ohio Open Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

1) All contracts, licenses, MOUs, MOAs, data agreements, letters of intent, State of Work documents, Scope of Work documents, and other agreement documents since Jan. 1, 2024 between Cleveland Police Department and Global Intelligence Inc. (also called Cybercheck or Cybercheck.AI) for the provision of the Cybercheck service (excluding any already undisclosed via previous FOIAs).

2) All email exchanges, including attachments, between Cleveland Police Department staff and email addresses with the domain "@cybercheck.ai" between Jan. 1, 2024 and the present (excluding any already undisclosed via previous FOIAs).

3) All email exchanges, including attachments, between staff of the Cleveland Police Department that reference Cybercheck or its provider Global Intelligence, sent between Jan. 1, 2023 and the present. 

I kindly request that you waive all fees as I am a journalist and the requested records will be used for journalism in the public interest.

If you choose to impose fees, I request a detailed breakdown of the fees, including the hourly wage of each employee involved and an explanation of the employee hours required to fulfill the request.

If you choose to reject this request or redact portions of responsive documents, I ask that you provide a detailed breakdown of the statutory exemptions and associated case law underlying your decision to withhold each/any portions from public review.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Jessica Hatcher

title: Password-Protected FOIA Authority (Immigration and Customs Enforcement)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Password-Protected FOIA Authority (Immigration and Customs Enforcement)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

In recent years, your agency FOIA Office has required FOIA requestors to access agency records using a government-created password that is unique to the file containing responsive documents. The FOIA Office accomplishes this  either through requiring FOIA requestors to utilize a 6-8 character password delivered by a second or separate communication.  

Practically speaking, this doubles the number of communications the agency must generate in turning over responsive documents, and prevents widespread public dissemination of the records by restricting their access only to the requestor receiving the password. Your agency is thus doubling the work it does at one point of the FOIA response process while reducing significantly the transmissibility of the public records it provides. This is occurring at a time when multi-year backlogs and court orders plague timely access to vital information about the agency’s function.  

All of this would be understandable and unavoidable if Congress required such actions. But it did not. The Freedom of Information Act and DHS’s FOIA regulations contain no language requiring public records to be password-protected. See 5 USC 552, and 6 CFR 5.1 et seq. Indeed, these authorities arguably prohibit such actions, because they constitute a conversion of the records from their original format into one constituting a completely new, password-protected document. 

Please provide any agency record documenting:  (a) the legal authority from which your FOIA Office claims it derives the power to place non-exempt public records responsive to FOIA requests into a password-protected document. 

(b) the Standard Operating Procedure used by your FOIA Office to assign non-exempt records a password. 

(c) any contract materials with any third-party reflecting monies paid by your agency to outside contractors for password-protecting FOIA documents. 

(d) any record reflecting the total time FOIA Office personnel dedicated during FY20, FY21 and FY22 (to date) to password-protecting records. 

(e) any governing policy denoting when password protection is appropriate, and when it is not. 

Because your agency’s FOIA Office suffers from longstanding, unabated backlogs and extended, multi-year processing times of the kind expressly condemned by Congress in amending the FOIA, because your agency’s password protection practice appears to have no basis in law, and because this practice represents a potential obstacle to every FOIA requestor, including those seeking expedited processing, this policy impacts the substantial due process rights of requestors, and raises serious questions as to your Office’s capacity to discharge its obligations under the law, threatening public confidence in its functioning, we respectfully request expedited processing pursuant to 6 CFR 5.5(e). 

Thank you,

Andrew Free
#DetentionKills Transparency Initiative 
Al Otro Lado

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Andrew Free

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request: Password-Protected FOIA Authority (Immigration and Customs Enforcement)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

I wanted to follow up on the following Freedom of Information Act request, copied below, and originally submitted on June 30, 2022. Please let me know when I can expect to receive a response.

Thanks for your help, and let me know if further clarification is needed.

communications.subject: ICE FOIA Request 2022-ICFO-22424
communications.communication:
July 05, 2022 Andrew Free MuckRock News DEPT MR 128122 263 Huntington Ave Boston, MA 02115 RE:     ICE FOIA Case Number 2022-ICFO-22424  Dear Mr. Free: This acknowledges receipt of your June 30, 2022, Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request to U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), for any agency record documenting: (a) the legal authority from which your FOIA Office claims it derives the power to place non-exempt public records responsive to FOIA requests into a password-protected document; (b) the Standard Operating Procedure used by your FOIA Office to assign non-exempt records a password; (c) any contract materials with any third-party reflecting monies paid by your agency to outside contractors for password-protecting FOIA documents; (d) any record reflecting the total time FOIA Office personnel dedicated during FY20, FY21 and FY22 (to date) to password-protecting records; (e) any governing policy denoting when password protection is appropriate, and when it is not.  Your request was received in this office on June 30, 2022. PLEASENOTE: Requesters seekingresponsive records from the U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) areencouraged to create a DHS FOIA Public Access Link (PAL) accountat: . Creating a PALaccount will allow you to directly submit your FOIA request to ICE and trackthe status of your request. In PAL, you can view your prior PAL submissions,sent correspondences, and responsive records. Although PAL is preferred, ICEFOIA will continue to accept FOIA requests via email at (https://foiarequest.dhs.gov/) https://foiarequest.dhs.gov or via regularmail at U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement, Freedom ofInformation Act Office, 500 12 ICE-FOIA@ice.dhs.gov th St. SW, STOP 5009, Washington,DC 20536-5009. Due to the increasing number of FOIA requests received by this office, we may encounter some delay in processing your request. Per Section 5.5(a) of the DHS FOIA regulations, 6 C.F.R. Part 5, ICE processes FOIA requests according to their order of receipt. Although ICE’s goal is to respond within 20 business days of receipt of your request, the FOIA does permit a 10-day extension of this time period. As your request seeks numerous documents that will necessitate a thorough and wide-ranging search, ICE will invoke a 10-day extension for your request, as allowed by Title 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(6)(B). If you care to narrow the scope of your request, please contact our office. We will make every effort to comply with your request in a timely manner. Provisions of the FOIA allow us to recover part of the cost of complying with your request.  We shall charge you for records in accordance with the DHS Interim FOIA regulations, as they apply to media requesters.  As a media requester, you will be charged 10 cents per page for duplication; the first 100 pages are free.  We will construe the submission of your request as an agreement to pay up to $25.00. You will be contacted before any further fees are accrued. We have queried the appropriate program offices within ICE for responsive records. If any responsive records are located, they will be reviewed for determination of releasability. Please be assured that one of the processors in our office will respond to your request as expeditiously as possible. We appreciate your patience as we proceed with your request. 
Your request has been assigned reference number 2022-ICFO-22424. Please refer to this identifier in any future correspondence. To check the status of an ICE FOIA/PA request, please visit . Please note that to check the status of a request, you must enter the 2022-ICFO-22424 tracking number. If you need any further assistance or would like to discuss any aspect of your request, please contact the FOIA office. You may send an e-mail to ice-foia@ice.dhs.gov, call toll free (866) 633-1182, or you may contact our FOIA Public Liaison, Marcus Francis, in the same manner. Additionally, you have a right to right to seek dispute resolution services from the Office of Government Information Services (OGIS) which mediates disputes between FOIA requesters and Federal agencies as a non-exclusive alternative to litigation. If you are requesting access to your own records (which is considered a Privacy Act request), you should know that OGIS does not have the authority to handle requests made under the Privacy Act of 1974. You may contact OGIS as follows: Office of Government Information Services, National Archives and Records Administration, 8601 Adelphi Road-OGIS, College Park, Maryland 20740-6001, e-mail at ogis@nara.gov; telephone at 202-741-5770; toll free at 1-877-684-6448; or facsimile at 202-741-5769. (http://www.dhs.gov/foia-status) http://www.dhs.gov/foia-status
Regards, 

ICE FOIA Office 
Immigration and Customs Enforcement 
Freedom of Information Act Office 
500 12th Street, S.W., Stop 5009 
Washington, D.C. 20536-5009 
Telephone: 1-866-633-1182 
Visit our FOIA website at www.ice.gov/foia

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Orangeburg County Sheriff)

communications.subject: South Carolina Public Records Law Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Orangeburg County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the South Carolina Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 15 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

title: Robert LaSardo's Navy personnel records

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Robert LaSardo's Navy personnel records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of the U.S. Coast Guard personnel records (OMPF) for a former servicemember named ROBERT LASARDO (DATE OF BIRTH: 09/20/1963). Mr. LaSardo was born in Brooklyn. NY. He served in the U.S. Navy for four years during the 1980s, likely during the first half of the decade. Reportedly, he spent the first two years handling attack dogs in the Aleutian Islands, at one point stationed on Adak Island. 

The public interest in Mr. LaSardo, a well known character actor in Hollywood since the late 1980s, should favor disclosure of many of these records, particularly in relation to any disciplinary records, should those records exist.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo

title: Public Records Request: Flock audits (Douglas County Sheriffs Office (NV))

communications.subject: Nevada Public Records Act Request: Public Records Request: Flock audits (Douglas County Sheriffs Office (NV))
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Nevada Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

3. Event Log in Flock
The Flock Safety Event Log for the time period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

e

title: Flock Safety ALPR Records Request - Riverside Police Department

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Flock Safety ALPR Records Request - Riverside Police Department
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting the following records related to Flock Safety automated license plate reader (ALPR) systems operated by or in partnership with the Riverside Police Department:

1. CONTRACTS/AGREEMENTS: All contracts, agreements, memoranda of understanding, amendments, renewals, or other legal instruments between the Riverside Police Department and Flock Safety, Inc., including pricing, terms of service, and data governance provisions.

2. CAMERA INVENTORY & GPS LOCATIONS: A complete list of all Flock Safety ALPR cameras deployed within your jurisdiction, including each camera's physical address and/or GPS coordinates, installation date, and operational status.

3. SEARCH AUDIT LOGS: All audit logs, access logs, or query records showing who accessed the Flock Safety system, when, what license plates were queried, and the stated reason or case number associated with each query.

4. NETWORK/DATA SHARING RECORDS: All records describing which agencies, entities, or networks the Riverside Police Department shares Flock Safety data with, including any Hot List sharing agreements, data sharing policies, and agency network participation records.

5. DETECTION/HIT STATISTICS: Aggregate statistics on the number of license plate reads, alerts, hits, or matches generated by Flock Safety ALPR cameras, broken down by time period if available.

6. CAMERA FOOTAGE/READS: All license plate read images, associated metadata (timestamp, location, plate number), and any video or image captures associated with the following individual and vehicle:
   - Name: Anthony Waldheim
   - California License Plate: 9HEE841
   Please search all available records within the system's retention period.

I request records in electronic format. If any records are withheld in whole or in part, please provide a written explanation citing the specific statutory exemption(s) relied upon for each withheld record or portion thereof.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Anthony Waldheim

title: Public Records Request — California Generative AI Records

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Public Records Request — California Generative AI Records
communications.communication:
Subject: Public Records Request — AI Chatbot Interaction Logs

April 9, 2026

Public Records Coordinator
California Department of Technology
P.O. Box 1810
Rancho Cordova, CA 95741

Dear Public Records Coordinator,

I am writing on behalf of a team of researchers and journalists from Stanford University, the University of Southern California, KNKX Public Radio and the New York Times. We are studying how government agencies are adopting and using AI tools in their daily work.

We are writing to request records related to California's use of generative AI tools by state employees, including the Poppy GenAI platform developed by the California Department of Technology.

We are requesting the following records under Cal. Gov. Code § 6253(c):

1. All interaction logs from Poppy, ChatGPT, Microsoft Copilot, Google Gemini, Claude, or any other generative AI chatbot tool — including prompts submitted by state employees and responses generated by the AI — for the period from September 25, 2025 through the date this request is processed. We request these records in their native electronic format where available (e.g., HTML exports, JSON, or CSV).

2. Any policies, guidelines, acceptable use policies, or training materials governing state employee use of Poppy or other generative AI chatbot tools.

3. Any data retention or deletion policies applicable to AI chatbot interaction logs, including any schedules for automatic deletion of interaction data.

4. Administrative usage statistics for Poppy showing number of users, sessions, and queries processed.

We are seeking the substantive content of AI interactions for research purposes. We do not need personally identifying information of individual employees (such as names and email addresses).

Poppy is an enterprise generative AI platform built by CDT for California state employees, utilizing 11 different AI models and grounded on public state data. As of early 2026, Poppy has more than 2,600 users across 66 departments, with over 20 state departments involved in its development and testing (see California Department of Technology, "Poppy: California's Digital Assistant," https://www.genai.ca.gov/poppy/; see also StateScoop, "Only California state employees can use Poppy, a new AI assistant designed for security," https://statescoop.com/california-poppy-state-government-ai-assistant/; GovTech, "California Looks to Drive Efficiency With AI Assistant," https://www.govtech.com/artificial-intelligence/california-looks-to-drive-efficiency-with-ai-assistant). Governor Newsom has also launched initiatives to accelerate responsible AI in state government (see Governor of California, Dec. 16, 2025, https://www.gov.ca.gov/2025/12/16/governor-newsom-launches-new-initiatives-to-partner-with-tech-policy-experts-and-accelerate-responsible-ai-in-state-government/).

We note that records of this type have been made available in response to public records requests in other jurisdictions. See Cascade PBS, "WA City Officials Are Using ChatGPT to Write Government Documents" (Aug. 2025), https://www.cascadepbs.org/news/2025/08/wa-city-officials-are-using-chatgpt-to-write-government-documents/; see also Texas State AI Usage — Texas Department of Transportation (TxDOT), MuckRock, https://www.muckrock.com/foi/texas-109/texas-state-ai-usage-texas-department-of-transportation-txdot-196227/#files.

The results of this research will be published in peer-reviewed academic venues and will contribute to public understanding of how public servants interact with AI systems. This request is made by an academic research institution for scholarly, non-commercial purposes; as such, we request a fee waiver or reduction. If a fee waiver is not granted, please inform me of estimated costs before proceeding. I am willing to pay fees up to $20.

I understand that this may be a novel type of records request and I am happy to answer any technical questions. Please feel free to contact me at the email address below.

Thank you for your time and assistance.

Sincerely,
Alexander Spangher
Postdoctoral Researcher
Stanford University
alexspan@stanford.edu

P.S. To facilitate your response, we note that ChatGPT conversation histories can be exported by individual users via Settings > Data Controls > Export Data. For Microsoft Copilot, interaction histories may be available through the Microsoft 365 admin console or the Purview compliance center. For Google Gemini, interaction data may be available through Google Vault or Google Takeout. We are happy to provide additional technical guidance if that would be helpful.

P.P.S. The California Public Records Act defines "public records" to include any writing containing information relating to the conduct of the public's business, regardless of physical form or characteristics, encompassing electronic communications and digital files. Cal. Gov. Code § 7920.530. Governor Newsom's Executive Order N-12-23 (September 2023) directed state agencies to develop a generative AI framework, and the CalGovOps GenAI Policy Framework (2024) requires agencies to maintain inventories of AI/GenAI tools and document use cases. Consistent with this trend nationally, NARA has made clear that chats and other electronic messages used for agency business are likely government records. NARA Bulletin 2023-02 (Jan. 5, 2023), https://www.archives.gov/records-mgmt/bulletins/2023/2023-02.

title: Dunwoody/Targeted Victory Communications

communications.subject: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Dunwoody/Targeted Victory Communications
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All emails, text messages, direct messages, meeting invitations, calendar entries, or other written communications between any employee, official, or representative of the City of Dunwoody (including but not limited to the Dunwoody Police Department, City Manager's office, Mayor's office, City Council members, and Technology Department) and any employee or representative of Targeted Victory, from January 1, 2026 through the present.

This request specifically includes but is not limited to communications with the following Targeted Victory email addresses that appeared on the February 12, 2026 RTCC tour invitation:
- cpartoyan@targetedvictory.com
- ehockenbury@targetedvictory.com
- abutler@targetedvictory.com

Pursuant to O.C.G.A. § 50-18-71(b)(1), I request that responsive records be provided in their native electronic format with full metadata preserved, including but not limited to message headers, timestamps, sender/recipient information, and attachment metadata.

If any portion of this request is denied, I request that you:
(1) Identify each responsive record being withheld with sufficient detail to permit me to assess the basis for withholding;
(2) Cite the specific statutory exemption(s) claimed for each record or portion thereof; and
(3) Pursuant to the segregability requirement of O.C.G.A. § 50-18-72(a)(44), produce all non-exempt portions of partially exempt records with redactions clearly marked and justified on a field-by-field basis.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jason Hunyar

title: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Crawford County)

communications.subject: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Crawford County)
communications.communication:
Dear Open Records FOIA Coordinator,

Pursuant to Michigan's Freedom of Information Act, MCL 15.231 et seq., I am requesting a copy of the Open Records county-wide assessment roll in its existing electronic format (CSV, DBF, or equivalent tabular export). If a single county-wide file does not exist, individual municipal-level exports that together cover all parcels in the county will satisfy this request.

Requested fields include, to the extent they exist in the record: parcel number, property address, owner name and mailing address, assessed value, state equalized value, taxable value, property classification, homestead/PRE status, school district, and any recorded sale date and sale price. I understand that many counties routinely provide similar datasets to commercial data aggregators such as Zillow and other real estate platforms, and I am requesting the same or comparable data.

I ask that this request be interpreted liberally, consistent with MCL 15.231(2). To the extent additional non-exempt fields exist in the record beyond those listed above, please include them. If the records are available for multiple years or assessment cycles, please include as broad a duration as is readily available rather than limiting the response to the current year alone. Where any question arises about whether to include or exclude non-exempt information, I respectfully ask that you err on the side of inclusion.

I am not requesting GIS data, parcel geometry, or any record that must be created — only a copy of existing electronic records. Please note that MCL 15.443(4) provides that the Enhanced Access to Public Records Act does not limit inspection and copying of public records under FOIA.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you.
Alexander Munro
Ypsilanti, MI
734-272-6612
awmunro@gmail.com

Hi Paul,

Thank you for getting back to me with the quote from Crawford Co.

Could you refer me to (1) Crawford's policy or ordinance defining this fee, and (2) to Crawford Co's appeals process as it pertains to FOIA appeals regarding fee structure (ie, does Crawford hold a hearing before their BoC or does a Crawford Co Administrator handle FOIA appeals internally)?

title: North Carolina Public Records Law Request: Employment Information (Alamance County Sheriff)

communications.subject: North Carolina Public Records Law Request: North Carolina Public Records Law Request: Employment Information (Alamance County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the North Carolina Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Employment information for all sworn law enforcement officers employed from the dates: 1950–present. Specifically, I request the following public information for each officer: Full name, Date of original employment/appointment, position title and history of position changes, dates of promotions, demotions, transfers. 

If possible, please provide this data in electronic format, Excel or CSV, database, via email attachment or CD-ROM if email is not feasible.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Alex Noll

communications.subject: North Carolina Public Records Law Request: North Carolina Public Records Law Request: Employment Information (Alamance County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
Dear Mr. Noll,

The County has received your request for employment information concerning sworn law enforcement officers employed from 1950 to present.

Under North Carolina law, certain personnel information is public, including employee name, date of original employment or appointment, current position/title, and the date and type of each promotion, demotion, transfer, suspension, separation, or other change in position classification.

The County will produce responsive public employment information that is currently maintained in its electronic systems.

The County does not maintain a single searchable electronic database of sworn law enforcement employment records dating back to 1950. Records prior to implementation of the County's current electronic HR system are not maintained in a consolidated, exportable format and would require manual file review and reconstruction. The North Carolina Public Records Law does not require a public agency to create new records or reconstruct historical data not maintained in its files.

Based on our current systems, the County is able to provide public employment information maintained electronically. Please let us know whether production of those existing electronic records will satisfy your request, or whether you wish to narrow or clarify the timeframe or scope.

If any costs are associated with production, we will notify you in advance.

With kind regards,
J Kwame Opata
Assistant County Attorney

[cid:image001.png@01DCAA63.C3AE8170]

files.source: Alamance County Sheriff, Alamance County, NC

Sincerely,

Alex Noll

communications.subject: RE: Request for Public Records - Andre Natta
communications.communication:
Good morning,
 Per Section 2-4 of the Tuscaloosa Municipal Code, the City is required to charge for staff time (beyond the first hour of staff time) spent researching and responding to a public records request. City staff has estimated that it will take about 12 hours of staff time to respond to your request. This includes time to research and gather potentially responsive records, to convert those files into a usable format (for example, converting .msg files to .pdf files), and reviewing the records to ensure they are responsive. We estimate the total charges for this staff time to be about $588.84. Please note: this is only an estimate and the total charges may be more or less depending on the length of time it takes to fulfill your requests. This also does not include the additional $10.00 if the responsive documents must be provided on a flash drive or CD/DVD.  Please let me know if you are willing to pay those charges, and we will begin working on the response ASAP.
Public Records

The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

title: Death Investigation Records: Alireza Pahlavi (Jan 4, 2011)

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Death Investigation Records: Alireza Pahlavi (Jan 4, 2011)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Regarding the death of Alireza Pahlavi (DOB: April 28, 1966), which occurred on or about January 4, 2011, at 100 West Newton St, Boston, MA.

Specifically, I am requesting copies of:

1. The full Police Incident Report (Form 1.1 or equivalent).

2. Any notes regarding a "Last Will and Testament" or "Suicide Note" found at the scene.

3. The Cremation Authorization Form signed by the next of kin.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

Thank you.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Iran Archive

communications.subject: RE: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request #B003701-122825
communications.communication:
To the Supervisor of Records:

I am appealing the response from the Boston Police Department regarding request #B003701-122825. The Department claimed they were "unable to find responsive documents" regarding the death investigation of Alireza Pahlavi (Jan 4, 2011).

This claim constitutes a "constructive denial" and is factually contested for the following reasons:

Public Record of Police Presence: On Jan 4, 2011, major news outlets (CNN, BBC, Boston Globe) reported that Boston Police officers physically responded to the scene at 100 West Newton St. to investigate the death.

Mandatory Reporting: BPD procedure mandates the filing of an "Incident Report" (Form 1.1) for any unattended death or suicide. It is procedurally impossible for officers to respond to a high-profile death without generating a single record.

Improper Search: Claiming "no records exist" suggests an inadequate search of the archives or that records were removed without citing the proper exemption.

Remedy Sought: I request that you order the Boston Police Department to conduct a renewed, thorough search for the Incident Report and, if the records were transferred to federal agencies (e.g., FBI), to provide a written statement confirming this transfer.

communications.communication:
Subject: SUPPLEMENTAL EVIDENCE: Public Record of Forensic Details - Appeal SPR26/0176

To the Supervisor of Records (Attn: Clare Eberman, Esq.):

I am submitting a contemporary legal analysis authored by Larry Klayman (former DOJ prosecutor), published shortly after the death of Alireza Pahlavi.

In this document, Mr. Klayman references specific forensic details regarding the weapon (shotgun) and the ballistic impossibility of the official narrative (simultaneous discharge of both barrels).

The existence of such detailed public discussion by a legal expert contradicts the Boston Police Department's claim that "no responsive records" exist. It is implausible that third-party legal experts possess more details about the crime scene than the police department's own archives. This suggests BPD's search was inadequate or records have been improperly removed.

Please consider this evidence in your determination.

Sincerely,
Iran Archive

Dear Attorney O'Donnell,

Re: Appeal SPR26/0176 (Boston Police Dept. Ref # B003701-122825)

I am writing to follow up on the Supervisor of Records' order dated February 2, 2026.
In your last correspondence, you explicitly stated: "The Department... will provide a response by February 13, 2026."

Today is February 19, and I have not received the mandated records or a valid update. The Department is now in violation of the Supervisor's order and its own committed deadline.

Please provide the requested records (Incident Report & Scene Notes) as soon as possible. If I do not receive a response or a valid legal justification by the end of the business day on Monday (February 23), I will be required to notify the Supervisor of Records of the Department's noncompliance and constructive denial of public records.

Sincerely,
Iran Archive

communications.subject: RE: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request #B003701-122825
communications.communication:
Dear Supervisor of Records,

I am writing to formally appeal the constructive denial and continued non-compliance by the Boston Police Department regarding my public records request (BPD Ref # B003701-122825).

On February 2, 2026, your office issued a clear order (SPR26/0176) directing the Department to review its files and respond. Following your order, Attorney Christine O'Donnell explicitly promised a response by February 13, 2026. The Department failed to meet its own deadline.

I subsequently granted them a good-faith extension until the end of the business day on Monday, February 23, 2026. As of today, February 24, the Department has completely ignored this final deadline. They have provided no responsive records, no valid exemption claims, and no request for further extension.

The Department is now in open defiance of the public records law and your specific administrative order. 

I respectfully request that you issue a new determination finding the Department in violation and immediately refer this matter to the Office of the Attorney General for enforcement action. 

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter.

Sincerely,
Iran Archive

communications.subject: RE: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request #SPR26/0622
communications.communication:
Subject: Failure to Comply with SPR Order - Request for Referral to the Attorney General

Dear Supervisor of Records,

I am writing to formally report that the Boston Police Department (BPD) has failed to comply with your recent determination regarding this Public Records request.

In your determination, you ordered the BPD to provide the responsive records within 10 days. That deadline has now officially passed, and the BPD has failed to produce any records, nor have they provided a legally sufficient explanation for their delay.

Because the Boston Police Department is now in direct violation of the Massachusetts Public Records Law and your explicit order, I respectfully request that your office refer this matter to the Office of the Attorney General for enforcement, pursuant to 950 CMR 32.09.

Thank you for your continued assistance in ensuring transparency and upholding the law.

Sincerely,
Iran Archive

communications.subject: RE: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request #B003701-122825
communications.communication:
Subject: Appeal - Inadequate Search by Boston Police Department (Ref: B003701-122825 / SPR26/0622)

Dear Supervisor of Records,

I am formally appealing the April 27, 2026 response from the Boston Police Department (BPD) regarding my public records request (B003701-122825) for the death investigation records of Alireza Pahlavi (January 4, 2011).

After months of delay and an explicit order from your office (SPR26/0622), BPD has now responded by claiming they were unable to find any responsive records.

This claim is procedurally impossible and constitutes a bad-faith failure to conduct an adequate search under the Public Records Law. The subject died of a gunshot wound (ruled suicide) at 100 West Newton St, Boston. BPD officers and detectives responded to the scene and conducted an investigation. It is a fundamental, non-negotiable law enforcement mandate to generate, at minimum, a Police Incident Report (Form 1.1) and an evidence log for any unnatural death involving a firearm.

BPD's assertion that absolutely no records exist for a highly publicized death investigation in their jurisdiction defies standard police protocols. This is a transparent administrative tactic designed to technically close the request while continuing to physically suppress the death scene evidence.

I respectfully request that the SPR:

Order the Boston Police Department to provide a detailed, written explanation of their search methodology, including the specific databases queried, the search terms used, and the identity of the personnel who conducted the search.

Compel BPD to explain the physical absence of mandatory incident reports for a confirmed 2011 firearm fatality.

Should BPD fail to provide a legally sufficient search or continue this obstruction, I request that your office formally refer this matter to the Attorney General's Division of Open Government for active enforcement.

Thank you.
Iran Archive

communications.communication:
RE: Public Records Request # B003701-122825 / SPR26/1593

To the Boston Police Department:

Thank you for your supplemental response dated May 8, 2026. However, I am writing to officially notify the Department that the links provided to the "Public Records Center" are non-functional, and the login system is failing to grant access to the requested records.

As the Supervisor of Public Records (SPR26/1593) closed my appeal based on your representation that the records were released, this ongoing technical barrier constitutes a failure to provide the records in an accessible format. The records are effectively still withheld from the requester.

To ensure compliance with the Massachusetts Public Records Law and resolve this matter immediately, I request that the Department bypass the faulty portal and upload the "Incident Report" directly to this MuckRock thread as a PDF attachment.

If the accessible records are not provided directly here within 48 hours, I will be forced to file a new appeal with the Supervisor of Records, citing a constructive denial of access through faulty technology.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter.
